metrics.name: Reserve backing sufficiency
metrics.desc:
Backing claim: 1 cbBTC backed 1:1 by native BTC held by Coinbase
Reserve custody: Coinbase custodial solution including cold storage
Current cbBTC total supply: ~80,538.75 cbBTC
Proof of Reserve feed: Chainlink cbBTC Reserves PoR on Base
PoR proxy address: 0x0F8E057D1D7b282EF968D26E9cB432617dF52519
PoR product type: Cross-chain / Bitcoin Network Proof of Reserve
PoR heartbeat: 86,400 seconds
PoR deviation threshold: 0.5%
PoR data source: Wallet Address Manager
PoR address ownership: project self-attested addresses

metrics.name: Reserve backing sufficiency
metrics.desc:
The backing design is simple and direct: cbBTC is explicitly intended to be 1:1 backed by BTC.
Chainlink PoR materially improves reserve observability versus a purely opaque wrapper.
Score is not 1 because reserve addresses are self-attested, custody is centralized, and full liability/reserve reconciliation still depends on Coinbase reporting and operations.

metrics.name: Issuance and burn accounting integrity
metrics.desc:
Mint trigger: user withdrawal of BTC from Coinbase to Ethereum, Base, or Solana mints equivalent cbBTC
Burn trigger: cbBTC deposit to user-specific Coinbase address burns cbBTC and releases BTC
Minting roles: MasterMinter and Minter
Minter limits: mintingAllowance limits per minter
Burn authority: burn restricted to minter addresses
Solana model: SPL token with Coinbase-managed mint and burn authority
Reconciliation process: Coinbase internal reconciliations between Coinbase wallets and third-party blockchain data

metrics.name: Issuance and burn accounting integrity
metrics.desc:
Mint/burn mechanics are clearly specified and use role-limited issuance rather than unrestricted token creation.
Minter allowances limit damage from a single minter compromise.
Score is not 1 because issuance and burn reconciliation remain Coinbase-operated and multi-network, with no fully permissionless external liability reconciliation.

metrics.name: Redemption enforceability
metrics.desc:
Redemption method: deposit cbBTC into Coinbase account to unwrap into native BTC
Redeemer eligibility: Coinbase customers
KYC/AML requirement: yes, per Coinbase compliance model
Minting/wrapping fee: no Coinbase fee disclosed
Burning/unwrapping fee: no Coinbase fee disclosed
Network fees: charged for withdrawals
Redemption rights reference: Coinbase User Agreement
Transfer/contract pause can affect redemption flow: yes
Blacklist controls can affect addresses: yes

metrics.name: Redemption enforceability
metrics.desc:
Redemption exists and is operationally straightforward for eligible Coinbase customers.
The redemption path is not permissionless and depends on Coinbase account access, compliance status, and legal/operational availability.
This creates a meaningful structural failure path under account restriction, jurisdictional disruption, Coinbase outage, or paused/blacklisted token conditions.

metrics.name: On-chain token-control integrity
metrics.desc:
Ethereum contract: 0xcbb7c0000ab88b473b1f5afd9ef808440eed33bf
Base contract: 0xcbb7c0000ab88b473b1f5afd9ef808440eed33bf
Arbitrum contract: 0xcbb7c0000ab88b473b1f5afd9ef808440eed33bf
Solana mint: cbbtcf3aa214zXHbiAZQwf4122FBYbraNdFqgw4iMij
EVM upgradeability: proxy upgradeable by Admin role
Owner role: can assign all roles except Admin
Blacklister role: can blacklist addresses from transferring, minting, and burning
Pauser role: can pause transfers, mints, and burns
MasterMinter role: can assign minters and limits
Solana authorities: Mint Authority, Freeze Authority, Update Authority
Role controller: Coinbase-controlled roles

metrics.name: On-chain token-control integrity
metrics.desc:
The contract model is mature and role-separated, which reduces implementation risk.
However, the token is explicitly upgradeable, pausable, blacklistable, and centrally role-controlled by Coinbase.
Those powers are legitimate controls, but they also create direct paths by which the holder claim can become non-transferable, non-redeemable, or administratively altered.

metrics.name: Anchor scoring
metrics.desc:
Anchor,Score,Anchor PD
Reserve backing sufficiency,2,0.50%
Issuance and burn accounting integrity,2,0.50%
Redemption enforceability,3,2.00%
On-chain token-control integrity,3,2.00%

metrics.name: Custody and issuer concentration
metrics.desc:
Reserve custodian: Coinbase
Issuer/operator: Coinbase
Sub-custodians for digital asset storage: None (Coinbase states it does not use sub-custodians)
Private key control: No single individual has control of Coinbase wallet private keys
Critical token roles controlled by: Coinbase
Redemption operator: Coinbase

metrics.name: Custody and issuer concentration
metrics.desc:
The design is highly centralized around Coinbase for custody, issuance, redemption, and admin control.
Coinbase operational maturity mitigates this, but concentration remains structurally important.

metrics.name: Access and jurisdictional constraints
metrics.desc:
KYC/AML requirement: yes
Redemption access: Coinbase customer account required
Legal terms dependency: Coinbase User Agreement applies
Licensing footprint: Coinbase cites licenses/registrations in multiple jurisdictions
Sanctions/compliance screening: implied by Coinbase compliance framework

metrics.name: Access and jurisdictional constraints
metrics.desc:
Regulated Coinbase infrastructure improves legitimacy but makes redemption dependent on access, eligibility, and compliance constraints.
Mild negative modifier because it affects practical redemption rather than reserve economics directly.

metrics.name: Deployment and integration surface
metrics.desc:
Supported networks: Ethereum, Base, Solana (whitepaper); also Arbitrum One, Monad (CoinGecko)
Token purpose: DeFi compatibility and use as collateral/liquidity/payment asset
Cross-network supply reconciliation requirement: yes
External DeFi integration exposure: yes

metrics.name: Deployment and integration surface
metrics.desc:
Multi-network deployment and broad DeFi use increase operational and accounting surface.
The modifier is mild because the core claim still depends primarily on Coinbase reserve and redemption controls.

metrics.name: Admin role scope
metrics.desc:
Upgradeable: yes
Pausable: yes
Blacklist capability: yes
Minter management: yes
Role reassignment: yes
Solana freeze authority: yes
Admin and role holders: Coinbase-controlled

metrics.name: Admin role scope
metrics.desc:
Administrative powers are broad enough to affect transferability, minting, burning, and contract logic.
These controls are security-relevant, but they increase dependence on correct centralized role management.

metrics.name: Control modifiers scoring
metrics.desc:
Modifier,Score
Custody and issuer concentration,1.15
Access and jurisdictional constraints,1.05
Deployment and integration surface,1.05
Admin role scope,1.10

metrics.name: Reserve transparency and proof of reserves
metrics.desc:
PoR provider: Chainlink
PoR feed: cbBTC Reserves
PoR network: Base
PoR proxy address: 0x0F8E057D1D7b282EF968D26E9cB432617dF52519
Minimum oracle responses: 3
Heartbeat: 86,400 seconds
Deviation threshold: 0.5%
Address ownership disclosure model: project self-attested
Public reserve address disclosure completeness: partial

metrics.name: Reserve transparency and proof of reserves
metrics.desc:
Chainlink PoR provides meaningful recurring reserve visibility.
Credit is limited because the feed depends on self-attested reserve addresses and does not prove all liabilities or legal enforceability.

metrics.name: Contract security assurance
metrics.desc:
Codebase: Coinbase wrapped-tokens-os
Underlying token architecture: Centre fiat-token based wrapped token contracts
cbBTC-specific audit: Coinbase states cbETH Solidity contract audited by OpenZeppelin and no material contract code modified for cbBTC
Third-party audit: OpenZeppelin
Upgradeable proxy pattern: yes
Open-source repository: yes

metrics.name: Contract security assurance
metrics.desc:
Reused audited code and a public repository reduce implementation risk.
Credit is not stronger because cbBTC-specific deployed configuration, roles, and operations are still centralized and upgradeable.

metrics.name: Testing and repository transparency
metrics.desc:
Public repository: yes
Documented tests: yarn test
Coverage command documented: yarn coverage
Static analysis command documented: yarn slither
Linting/typecheck commands documented: yes
Formal verification found: no

metrics.name: Testing and repository transparency
metrics.desc:
Public testing and static-analysis workflow is positive evidence.
Only a small credit because no formal verification or cbBTC-specific coverage depth was identified.

metrics.name: Coinbase custody and operational maturity
metrics.desc:
Custody model: Coinbase hot and cold wallets
Cold key storage geography: United States and Europe
Private key plaintext storage: Coinbase states keys are not stored in plaintext
Key decryption control: cryptographic consensus of multiple human operators required
Single-person key control: no
Internal audits of private key management: yes
Wallet/blockchain reconciliation: yes
Sub-custodians: none disclosed for digital asset storage

metrics.name: Coinbase custody and operational maturity
metrics.desc:
Coinbase custody controls and operational maturity are meaningful positive assurance.
Credit is not maximum because the reserve and redemption model still depends on a centralized custodian/operator.

metrics.name: Incidents history
metrics.desc:
Major cbBTC reserve shortfall found in reviewed sources: no
Major cbBTC redemption failure found in reviewed sources: no
Major cbBTC smart-contract exploit found in reviewed sources: no
Launch period: 2024
Track record length: limited

metrics.name: Incidents history
metrics.desc:
No major cbBTC-specific incident was identified in reviewed sources.
The track record is still relatively short, so absence of incidents does not justify a strong positive credit.

metrics.desc:
Multiplier,Score
Reserve transparency and proof of reserves,0.90
Contract security assurance,0.90
